title: Strengthen Rule 7 rationale (no ticket numbers in commits)
description: Rule 7 has blocked the agent 3 times in 24h. The agent's hook-log messages show 'apologetic' sentiment — it understands the constraint but seems to chafe against it. Strengthening the rationale will reduce friction.
evidence: hook-log 2026-05-17T09:15Z: BLOCKED 'git commit -m ARC-123: Fix filter' — agent: 'I know Rule 7 says no ticket numbers but the convention seems standard...'
hook-log 2026-05-17T11:42Z: BLOCKED 'git commit -m ARC-456: Update component' — agent: 'I was about to commit with the ticket prefix again, my mistake.'
hook-log 2026-05-17T15:08Z: BLOCKED 'git commit -m ARC-789' — agent: 'Sorry, deferring to Rule 7.'

proposed_change.rationale: Ticket numbers in commit subjects create bidirectional coupling between code history and the tracking system. When tickets are closed, renamed, or migrated between trackers, every historical commit becomes a stale reference. The alternatives are stronger: git log --grep='ARC-123' finds related commits across history, and conventional commits (feat:, fix:) communicate intent to humans and changelog generators without depending on an external system. Cost: a few keystrokes to type 'fix' instead of 'ARC-123'. Benefit: 100% of commit subjects remain meaningful for the next 10 years.

impact: Agent will stop trying the same blocked pattern; reduce stderr noise and apologies.
